document_title: Stateful Hash-Based Signature State Management
short_description: Guidance on managing stateful HBS (LMS/XMSS) state to prevent catastrophic key reuse. Covers state persistence and distributed signing strategies.
ProtocolOrToolImpact: Firmware signing; Secure boot; Code signing

document_title: Post-quantum Digital Signatures with ML-DSA in IKEv2
short_description: Defines ML-DSA (FIPS 204) for IKE_AUTH phase digital signatures in IKEv2. Companion to draft-ietf-ipsecme-ikev2-mlkem. Enables fully post-quantum IKEv2 authentication.

document_title: ML-DSA for TLS 1.3
short_description: Defines ML-DSA (FIPS 204) signature algorithms for use in TLS 1.3 authentication. Enables post-quantum certificate-based authentication in TLS alongside ML-KEM key exchange.

document_title: Use of the RSA-KEM Algorithm in the Cryptographic Message Syntax (CMS)
short_description: Defines RSA-KEM usage in CMS EnvelopedData using KEMRecipientInfo. Obsoletes RFC 5990. Supports hybrid RSA+ML-KEM migration scenarios for S/MIME.

document_title: Secure/Multipurpose Internet Mail Extensions (S/MIME) Version 4.0 Message Specification
short_description: S/MIME 4.0 email security standard for signing and encrypting email using CMS. Foundation for PQC email migration via RFC 9629 (KEM) and RFC 9882 (ML-DSA).

document_title: SP 800-90A Rev. 1: Recommendation for Random Number Generation Using Deterministic Random Bit Generators
short_description: NIST standard defining three approved DRBG mechanisms (CTR_DRBG, Hash_DRBG, HMAC_DRBG) for generating pseudorandom bits from seed entropy. All mechanisms use symmetric primitives and are quantum-safe.
ProtocolOrToolImpact: Random number generation; Key generation; Nonce generation

document_title: SP 800-90B: Recommendation for the Entropy Sources Used for Random Bit Generation
short_description: NIST standard for validating entropy sources used in random bit generators. Defines health tests (repetition count, adaptive proportion), min-entropy estimation methods, and IID testing requirements.
ProtocolOrToolImpact: Entropy source validation; CMVP ESV program
ToolchainSupport: Hardware RNG vendors; FIPS module developers

document_title: SP 800-90C: Recommendation for Random Bit Generator (RBG) Constructions
short_description: NIST standard specifying constructions for Random Bit Generators (RBGs) that combine entropy sources with DRBGs. Defines RBG1, RBG2, RBG3, and RBGC classes for defense-in-depth randomness.
ProtocolOrToolImpact: RBG construction; Combining entropy sources; Defense-in-depth

document_title: ETSI GS QKD 002 - QKD Use Cases
short_description: Use cases for quantum key distribution including government communications and financial networks.

document_title: ETSI GS QKD 003 - Components and Internal Interfaces
short_description: Defines QKD system components and their internal interfaces for interoperability.

short_description: Specifies the application programming interface for accessing QKD-generated keys from applications.

document_title: ETSI GS QKD 005 - Security Proofs
short_description: Framework for security proofs of QKD protocols including BB84 and related variants.

document_title: ETSI GS QKD 008 - QKD Module Security Specification
short_description: Security requirements and evaluation criteria for QKD modules analogous to Common Criteria.

document_title: ETSI GS QKD 011 - Component Characterization
short_description: Methods for characterizing individual QKD components such as single-photon sources and detectors.

document_title: ETSI GS QKD 012 - Device and Communication Channel Parameters
short_description: Control interface specification for QKD device parameters and communication channel monitoring.

document_title: ETSI GS QKD 014 - Protocol and Data Format for REST-based Key Delivery API
short_description: RESTful API specification for key delivery between QKD nodes and applications. Widely implemented reference API.

document_title: ETSI GS QKD 015 - Control Interface for Software Defined Networks
short_description: Defines the control interface between QKD network layer and SDN controllers for programmable quantum networks.

document_title: ETSI GS QKD 016 - Orchestration Interface of Software Defined Networks
short_description: Orchestration interface for managing QKD resources in SDN environments including multi-domain key routing.

document_title: ETSI GS QKD 018 - Orchestration Interface of Software Defined Networks
short_description: Orchestration interface for managing QKD resources in SDN environments. Defines YANG data models for interoperability between QKD network layer, SDN controllers, and orchestrators. Enables multi-domain key routing and programmable quantum networks.

document_title: ISO/IEC 23837-1:2023 - Security Requirements for Quantum Key Distribution (Part 1)
short_description: Security requirements for QKD modules. Defines protection profiles and security targets analogous to Common Criteria for QKD hardware.

document_title: ISO/IEC 23837-2:2023 - Evaluation and Testing of QKD Modules (Part 2)
short_description: Testing methodology and evaluation criteria for QKD modules. Provides test vectors and conformance assessment procedures.
applicable_industries: Testing Labs; QKD Vendors; Certification Bodies

document_title: Importing External Pre-Shared Keys (PSKs) for TLS 1.3
short_description: RFC 9258 defines an external PSK importer interface for TLS 1.3 (RFC 8446). It allows QKD-derived keys delivered via ETSI GS QKD 014 to be bound to a specific KDF and hash algorithm and imported as a TLS 1.3 pre-shared key — enabling QKD integration without any TLS protocol modifications.
ProtocolOrToolImpact: TLS 1.3 PSK; QKD key injection into TLS handshake

document_title: Recommendation for Key Derivation Using Pseudorandom Functions (Revision 1)
short_description: NIST SP 800-108 Rev.1 specifies key derivation functions (KDFs) based on pseudorandom functions including counter mode feedback mode and double-pipeline mode using HMAC or CMAC. Directly implemented in PKCS#11 v3.0 as CKM_SP800_108_COUNTER_KDF and used in HSM-based QKD key derivation pipelines.
ProtocolOrToolImpact: HSM key derivation from QKD secret; PKCS#11 CKM_SP800_108_COUNTER_KDF
ToolchainSupport: HSM vendors (Thales; Utimaco; nCipher); OpenSSL KBKDF

document_title: Recommendation for Key-Derivation Methods in Key-Establishment Schemes (Revision 2)
short_description: NIST SP 800-56C Rev.2 specifies one-step and extraction-then-expansion (EtE) key derivation methods for key-establishment schemes. The EtE approach supports hybrid shared secrets combining classical DH or PQC KEM output with a QKD-sourced entropy value — directly applicable to quantum-hybrid key exchange architectures.
ProtocolOrToolImpact: Hybrid key derivation (classical + QKD); TLS 1.3 key schedule

document_title: PKCS #11 Cryptographic Token Interface Standard Version 3.0
short_description: PKCS#11 v3.0 is the HSM programming interface standard. It adds CKM_HKDF_DERIVE and CKM_HKDF_KEY_GEN mechanisms for TLS 1.3 and CKM_SP800_108_COUNTER_KDF / FEEDBACK_KDF / DOUBLE_PIPELINE_KDF for NIST SP 800-108 derivation. Essential for HSM-based QKD integration workflows where QKD secrets are imported as non-extractable keys and used to derive session keys.
ProtocolOrToolImpact: HSM key derivation; PKCS#11 CKM_SP800_108_COUNTER_KDF; CKM_HKDF_DERIVE
ToolchainSupport: Thales Luna; Utimaco SecurityServer; nCipher nShield; SoftHSM

document_title: BIP-32: Hierarchical Deterministic Wallets
short_description: Defines HD wallet key derivation from a single seed enabling deterministic generation of unlimited key pairs.

document_title: BIP-39: Mnemonic Code for Generating Deterministic Keys
short_description: Standard for encoding entropy as a human-readable mnemonic phrase for wallet seed backup and recovery.
authors_or_organization: Marek Palatinus; Pavol Rusnak; Aaron Voisine; Sean Bowe
ProtocolOrToolImpact: HD Wallet seed backup; mnemonic recovery

document_title: BIP-44: Multi-Account Hierarchy for Deterministic Wallets
short_description: Defines multi-coin and multi-account HD wallet derivation path structure across blockchains.
ProtocolOrToolImpact: HD Wallet path standardisation; multi-chain support

document_title: SLIP-0010: Universal Private Key Derivation from Master Private Key
short_description: Extends BIP-32 to support Ed25519 and other non-secp256k1 curves for HD derivation; required for Solana.
ProtocolOrToolImpact: HD Wallet derivation for Ed25519; Solana key management

document_title: EIP-55: Mixed-case Checksum Address Encoding
short_description: Ethereum standard for checksummed mixed-case hex address encoding using Keccak-256 to detect typos.

document_title: EIP-155: Simple Replay Attack Protection
short_description: Introduces chain ID into Ethereum transaction signing to prevent cross-chain replay attacks.

document_title: SEC 2 v2: Recommended Elliptic Curve Domain Parameters
short_description: SECG standard defining domain parameters for secp256k1 and other elliptic curves used by Bitcoin and Ethereum.
ProtocolOrToolImpact: Bitcoin ECDSA; Ethereum ECDSA; secp256k1 parameters

document_title: RFC 8032: Edwards-Curve Digital Signature Algorithm (EdDSA)
short_description: IETF standard for EdDSA including Ed25519 and Ed448. Used by Solana. Deterministic nonce eliminates nonce-reuse vulnerabilities.
applicable_industries: Finance; IT; Digital Assets; Web3; Secure Messaging
ProtocolOrToolImpact: Solana transaction signing; Ed25519 digital signatures

document_title: Bitcoin: A Peer-to-Peer Electronic Cash System
short_description: Satoshi Nakamoto foundational paper introducing Bitcoin and blockchain: proof-of-work consensus and ECDSA-based ownership.

document_title: Ethereum: A Secure Decentralised Generalised Transaction Ledger
short_description: Formal specification of the Ethereum Virtual Machine by Gavin Wood. Defines Keccak-256 address derivation and ECDSA signing.
ProtocolOrToolImpact: Ethereum protocol; EVM; smart contract execution

document_title: OpenSSL 3.x Documentation
short_description: Official documentation for OpenSSL 3.x including FIPS provider command-line tools API reference and PQC provider guidance.
applicable_industries: IT; Software Development; Digital Assets; Finance
ProtocolOrToolImpact: TLS; X.509; ECDSA; EdDSA; PQC provider
ToolchainSupport: OpenSSL CLI; OpenSSL libssl; OpenSSL libcrypto

document_title: BIP-141: Segregated Witness (Consensus Layer)
short_description: Activates Segregated Witness on Bitcoin separating signature data (witness) from transaction inputs. Introduces SegWit witness versioning (v0 P2WPKH/P2WSH, v1 Taproot). BIP-360 (P2QRH) introduces SegWit v3 (bc1r...) as a quantum-resistant output type building directly on this witness versioning scheme.
authors_or_organization: Eric Lombrozo; Johnson Lau; Pieter Wuille (Bitcoin Core)
ProtocolOrToolImpact: Bitcoin witness versioning; transaction malleability fix; SegWit v0/v1/v3 foundation
ToolchainSupport: Bitcoin Core; Lightning Network; Ledger; Trezor

document_title: BIP-340: Schnorr Signatures for secp256k1
short_description: Defines 64-byte Schnorr signatures for secp256k1 with provable security, batch verification, and key aggregation (MuSig2). Required foundation for Taproot (BIP-341). BIP-360 (P2QRH) targets Taproot's Schnorr key-spend path as the primary quantum vulnerability to replace with post-quantum signatures.
authors_or_organization: Pieter Wuille; Jonas Nick; Tim Ruffing (Bitcoin Core)
ProtocolOrToolImpact: Bitcoin signature scheme; Taproot foundation; quantum exposure surface

document_title: BIP-341: Taproot: SegWit Version 1 Spending Rules
short_description: Activates Taproot on Bitcoin (block 709632). Defines SegWit v1 spending with a Schnorr key-spend path and Merklized Abstract Syntax Tree (MAST) script-spend path. BIP-360 (P2QRH) specifically removes the key-spend path as quantum-vulnerable under Shor's algorithm and proposes a SegWit v3 (bc1r...) replacement.
authors_or_organization: Pieter Wuille; Jonas Nick; Anthony Towns (Bitcoin Core)
ProtocolOrToolImpact: Bitcoin Taproot upgrade; Schnorr key-spend quantum vulnerability; BIP-360 context

document_title: Bitcoin BIP-360: Pay to Quantum Resistant Hash (P2QRH)
short_description: Proposes a new Bitcoin output type (SegWit v3, bc1r...) using post-quantum signatures via a soft fork. Removes the quantum-vulnerable key-spend path from Taproot. Exact PQC algorithms to be standardized in a companion BIP; ML-DSA and FALCON-512 are leading candidates under community discussion. Authored by Hunter Beast to address the HNFL risk to Bitcoin public keys.

document_title: EIP-4337: Account Abstraction Using Alt Mempool
short_description: Enables smart contract wallets on Ethereum without consensus-layer changes. As a PQC migration path, EIP-4337 smart accounts can verify ML-DSA or FALCON signatures on mainnet today — no protocol change required.
authors_or_organization: Vitalik Buterin; Yoav Weiss; Ethereum Foundation
ProtocolOrToolImpact: Ethereum wallet; smart account; PQC migration path

document_title: EIP-7702: Set Code for EOA
short_description: Allows EOAs to set executable code via signed authorisation tuples (Pectra hard fork). Provides a bridge for PQC migration: a delegated smart contract can verify ML-DSA or FALCON signatures on behalf of the EOA, enabling quantum-safe signing without full account migration. Note: EIP-7702 authorisation tuples are themselves signed with secp256k1; quantum resistance is provided by the delegated contract's verification logic.
authors_or_organization: Vitalik Buterin; Sam Wilson; Ansgar Dietrichs; Ethereum Foundation
ProtocolOrToolImpact: Ethereum EOA; smart account; Pectra hard fork

document_title: Harvest Now Decrypt Later: Post-Quantum Cryptography and Data Privacy Risks for Distributed Ledger Networks
short_description: Federal Reserve FEDS Working Paper (2025-093) analysing HNFL risks for blockchain networks. Examines how quantum computers could retroactively compromise immutable on-chain transaction data across Bitcoin, Ethereum, and distributed ledger systems.

document_title: Tasklist for Post-Quantum Ethereum
short_description: Ethereum Foundation research post outlining the full roadmap to post-quantum Ethereum. Covers STARK-based and lattice-based signature replacement for ECDSA and BLS12-381, and describes the emergency quantum fork path for an unplanned Q-Day.
ProtocolOrToolImpact: Ethereum protocol; consensus layer; PQC migration

document_title: Merkle Tree Certificates for TLS (draft-ietf-plants-merkle-tree-certs-01)
short_description: IETF Internet-Draft defining Merkle Tree Certificates (MTCs) for TLS, adopted by the IETF PLANTS working group as draft-ietf-plants-merkle-tree-certs-01 (February 2026). Replaces individual per-certificate CA signatures with a single signed Merkle tree root and compact inclusion proofs, reducing PQC TLS handshake authentication data by 63-74%.

document_title: Certificate Transparency Version 2.0
short_description: IETF Standards Track RFC defining Certificate Transparency Version 2.0. Obsoletes RFC 6962. Foundational for understanding the transparency log model that Merkle Tree Certificates extend for post-quantum TLS optimization. Specifies TLS extensions for CT log artifacts and certificate inclusion proofs.

document_title: Keeping the Internet fast and secure: introducing Merkle Tree Certificates
short_description: Cloudflare blog post explaining the Merkle Tree Certificate proposal, its motivation (PQC certificate bloat), architecture (MTCA, transparency service, subscribers), and experimental deployment results. Includes size comparison data.

document_title: Implementation and Analysis of Merkle Tree Certificates for Post-Quantum Secure Authentication in TLS
short_description: Master's thesis from Radboud University providing a practical implementation and performance analysis of Merkle Tree Certificates in TLS 1.3. Evaluates tree construction, proof generation, and handshake overhead with ML-DSA.

document_title: State of the Post-Quantum Internet in 2025
short_description: Cloudflare's annual review of PQC adoption across the Internet, covering ML-KEM deployment in TLS 1.3, browser support (Chrome, Firefox), certificate transparency challenges, and the MTC proposal as a solution to PQ certificate bloat.

document_title: eIDAS 2.0 Regulation (EU 2024/1183)
short_description: EU regulation establishing the European Digital Identity framework mandating EUDI Wallets for all member states by late 2026. Requires private sector acceptance by late 2027. Introduces Qualified Electronic Attestations of Attributes (QEAA) and specifies the trust framework for digital identity across EU. Supersedes eIDAS 1.0.
authors_or_organization: European Parliament; Council of the European Union
ProtocolOrToolImpact: EUDI Wallet; OpenID4VCI; OpenID4VP; QES; Digital Identity

document_title: EUDI Wallet Architecture and Reference Framework (ARF)
short_description: Technical architecture and reference framework for the European Digital Identity Wallet. Defines credential formats (mso_mdoc per ISO 18013-5 and SD-JWT VC per RFC 9901) trust framework protocol requirements (OpenID4VCI OpenID4VP) and cryptographic security requirements. Specifies PQC migration roadmap: Dec 2026 national roadmaps Dec 2030 high-risk migration Dec 2035 full transition.
ProtocolOrToolImpact: EUDI Wallet; mso_mdoc; SD-JWT VC; OpenID4VCI; OpenID4VP; Trust Framework

document_title: ISO/IEC 18013-5:2021 — Mobile Driving Licence (mDL) Application
short_description: International standard defining the mso_mdoc credential format for mobile driving licences and identity documents. Specifies binary CBOR encoding document structure namespace (eu.europa.ec.eudi.pid.1 for EUDI PID) and cryptographic protection via Mobile Security Objects (MSO) signed by the issuer. Foundational for EUDI Wallet PID credentials and proximity presentations via NFC and BLE.
ProtocolOrToolImpact: mso_mdoc; EUDI Wallet PID; mDL; Mobile Identity; Proximity Presentation

document_title: RFC 9901 — SD-JWT-based Verifiable Credentials (SD-JWT VC)
short_description: IETF RFC defining SD-JWT-based Verifiable Credentials format (vc+sd-jwt). Specifies credential structure selective disclosure mechanism claims key binding JWT and the holder binding proof. Adopted by EUDI Wallet ARF for online attestations (education diplomas professional qualifications). Text-based JSON encoding optimised for remote online services.
ProtocolOrToolImpact: SD-JWT VC; EUDI Wallet Attestations; Verifiable Credentials; Selective Disclosure

document_title: Selective Disclosure for JWTs (SD-JWT) — draft-ietf-oauth-selective-disclosure-jwt
short_description: IETF specification defining the selective disclosure mechanism for JWT claims using salted SHA-256 hashes. Enables minimal disclosure (only requested attributes revealed) and unlinkability (batch-issued credentials prevent correlation). Core privacy-by-design mechanism used in SD-JWT VC credentials for the EUDI Wallet ecosystem.
ProtocolOrToolImpact: Selective Disclosure; SD-JWT; EUDI Wallet Privacy; Data Minimization; Unlinkability

document_title: OpenID for Verifiable Credential Issuance (OpenID4VCI)
short_description: OpenID Foundation specification defining how credential issuers expose APIs for digital credential issuance. Specifies credential offer flow pre-authorisation code flow authorisation code flow and credential endpoint. Defines proof of possession using key binding JWTs. Mandatory protocol for all EUDI Wallet credential issuers (PID providers attestation issuers).
ProtocolOrToolImpact: EUDI Wallet Credential Issuance; OpenID4VCI; mso_mdoc; SD-JWT VC; Key Binding

document_title: OpenID for Verifiable Presentations (OpenID4VP)
short_description: OpenID Foundation specification defining how holders present verifiable credentials to relying parties. Specifies the authorization request presentation definition (DIF PE) and presentation submission. Enables selective disclosure presentations where holders reveal only the credential attributes required by the relying party. Mandatory for EUDI Wallet credential presentation flows.
ProtocolOrToolImpact: EUDI Wallet Credential Presentation; OpenID4VP; Selective Disclosure; Relying Party Verification

document_title: OAuth Status List — draft-ietf-oauth-status-list
short_description: IETF specification for a compact bit-array status list to check validity states of credentials (valid revoked suspended). Provides privacy-preserving revocation: the verifier cannot determine which specific credential is being checked from the status list position alone. Used in EUDI Wallet ecosystem for credential revocation checking without revealing holder identity to the issuer.
ProtocolOrToolImpact: Credential Revocation; EUDI Wallet; SD-JWT VC; Privacy-Preserving Status

document_title: Security of the EUDI Wallet Architecture — ENISA
short_description: ENISA analysis of security risks and mitigations for the European Digital Identity Wallet architecture. Identifies EUDI Wallet providers as high-impact targets for early PQC adoption. Covers security requirements for credential formats trust framework key management (WSCD/WSCA) and protocol security. Provides recommendations for member state EUDI Wallet implementations.
ProtocolOrToolImpact: EUDI Wallet Security; PQC Transition; Digital Identity Risk; WSCD; WSCA

document_title: Cloud Signature Consortium API v2 Specification
short_description: Cloud Signature Consortium API v2 for remote digital signature services. Defines endpoints for credential listing (/csc/v2/credentials/list) credential information (/csc/v2/credentials/info) authorization (/csc/v2/credentials/authorize) and hash signing (/csc/v2/signatures/signHash). Supports Qualified Electronic Signatures (QES) via remote HSMs. Adopted by eIDAS trust service providers (QTSPs) for EUDI Wallet-compatible SCAL2 signature services.
ProtocolOrToolImpact: QES; Remote Digital Signature; EUDI Wallet QTSP; CSC API; SCAL2

document_title: ETSI EN 319 411 — Requirements for Trust Service Providers Issuing Certificates
short_description: ETSI standard defining policy and security requirements for Qualified Trust Service Providers (QTSPs) issuing qualified certificates under eIDAS. Forms the basis for European CA auditing under WebTrust/ETSI audit schemes and the EUDI Trust Framework for credential issuer accreditation. Part 1 covers general requirements; Part 2 covers NCP/NCP+/QCP/eIDAS policies.
ProtocolOrToolImpact: QTSP Requirements; QES; European PKI; EUDI Trust Framework; CA Auditing

document_title: NIST SP 800-63-3 — Digital Identity Guidelines
short_description: NIST framework for digital identity management. SP 800-63A covers identity proofing and enrollment (Identity Assurance Levels IAL1/2/3). SP 800-63B covers authentication (Authenticator Assurance Levels AAL1/2/3). SP 800-63C covers federation (Federation Assurance Levels FAL1/2/3). Referenced in EUDI Wallet implementations for IAL/AAL equivalency mapping with European LoA (Low Substantial High).
ProtocolOrToolImpact: Digital Identity Assurance; IAL; AAL; FAL; Federation; Identity Proofing

document_title: Transitioning the Use of Cryptographic Algorithms and Key Lengths (Rev 3)
short_description: Algorithm deprecation roadmap: SHA-1 and RSA/ECC <128-bit disallowed after 2030. Establishes federal algorithm retirement calendar aligned with PQC transition. References FIPS 203/204/205 as post-2030 replacements.

document_title: Implementation of Quantum Safe Ecosystem in India — Report of the Task Force
short_description: India's national PQC migration roadmap from the Department of Science & Technology. Sets 2027–2029 migration timelines for Critical Information Infrastructure. Recommends hybrid PQC+QKD approach with industry-specific deadlines. First major non-Western official quantum-safe roadmap.

document_title: Why quantum security is a question leaders cannot ignore
short_description: World Economic Forum perspective for C-suite and board audiences on quantum security strategic risk. Frames PQC migration as a board-level priority with competitive and regulatory dimensions. Part of WEF's ongoing series on quantum readiness for business leaders.

document_title: SP 800-22 Rev. 1a: A Statistical Test Suite for Random and Pseudorandom Number Generators for Cryptographic Applications
short_description: NIST statistical test suite defining 15 statistical tests (Monobit, Runs, Chi-Squared, DFT, Serial, and more) for evaluating randomness quality of bit sequences produced by cryptographic RBGs. Used for validating entropy source output quality prior to NIST ESV submission.
ProtocolOrToolImpact: Entropy testing; RBG validation; Entropy source qualification

document_title: AIS 20/AIS 31: Functionality Classes and Evaluation Methodology for Deterministic and Physical Random Number Generators
short_description: BSI (Germany) standard defining functionality classes DRG.1–DRG.4 for deterministic RNGs and PTG.1–PTG.3 for physical/true RNGs. AIS 31 compliance is required for Common Criteria RNG evaluations and is cited by QRNG hardware certifications including ID Quantique Quantis.
ProtocolOrToolImpact: TRNG/DRBG certification; QRNG hardware validation; Common Criteria
ToolchainSupport: Common Criteria; QRNG hardware; HSM firmware

document_title: 2024 Quantum Threat Timeline Report
short_description: Annual expert survey projecting the probability and timeline of a cryptographically-relevant quantum computer (CRQC). Projects 5–15% probability by 2030 and 50% by 2034–2039. Foundational source for Mosca's Theorem and HNDL/HNFL risk calculations.

document_title: Cryptographic Message Syntax (CMS)
short_description: Core CMS standard defining SignedData, EnvelopedData, and AuthEnvelopedData structures. Foundation for S/MIME email signing and encryption. All PQC CMS RFCs (9629, 9882, 9814, 9708, 9690) extend this format.
applicable_industries: Email; Document Signing; Code Signing; PKI
ProtocolOrToolImpact: S/MIME; CMS; document signing; email encryption

document_title: Using Advanced Encryption Standard (AES) Counter with CBC-MAC (AES-CCM) and AES Galois/Counter Mode (AES-GCM) Algorithms in the Cryptographic Message Syntax (CMS)
short_description: Defines AuthEnvelopedData content type for AEAD-based encryption in CMS. The authenticated-enveloped structure with AES-GCM provides both confidentiality and integrity — the preferred encryption mode for PQC email migration via RFC 9629.

document_title: Internet Key Exchange Protocol Version 2 (IKEv2)
short_description: Defines IKEv2 for establishing IPsec Security Associations. Foundational protocol for VPN key exchange; all PQC IKEv2 extensions (RFC 9370, draft-ietf-ipsecme-ikev2-mlkem) build on this specification.
applicable_industries: VPN; IPsec; Enterprise; Government; Critical Infrastructure
ToolchainSupport: StrongSwan; Libreswan; Cisco IOS; Juniper Junos

document_title: Use of Curve25519 and Curve448 in the Secure Shell (SSH) Protocol
short_description: Defines curve25519-sha256 and curve448-sha512 key exchange for SSH. The classical KEX baseline that mlkem768x25519-sha256 hybrid extends for post-quantum SSH in OpenSSH 9.9+.

document_title: Rosenpass: Formally Verified Post-Quantum Protocol for WireGuard
short_description: Defines the Rosenpass protocol for adding PQC to WireGuard. Runs a separate ML-KEM-768 + Classic McEliece handshake and injects the combined shared secret as a WireGuard pre-shared key (PSK). Formally verified with ProVerif.

document_title: NSA Quantum Key Distribution and Quantum Cryptography
short_description: NSA advisory explaining why it does not recommend QKD for national security systems. States that QKD addresses only the key distribution problem, requires special-purpose equipment, introduces trusted node vulnerabilities, and has side-channel risks in real implementations. Recommends PQC (CNSA 2.0 algorithms) as the primary solution. Key reference for balanced QKD vs PQC evaluation.

document_title: The Secure Shell (SSH) Transport Layer Protocol
short_description: Defines the SSH transport layer including key exchange, server authentication, and encryption. The key exchange phase is where QKD-derived pre-shared keys can be injected via the ext-info mechanism or future SSH KEX extensions. Foundational standard for understanding QKD integration into SSH.

document_title: IEEE 802.1AE-2018: MAC Security (MACsec)
short_description: Defines MACsec — IEEE Layer 2 encryption standard for Ethernet networks. MACsec uses AES-GCM-128/256 for confidentiality and integrity. QKD-derived keys can be injected as Secure Association Keys (SAKs) via the MACsec Key Agreement (MKA) protocol, making MACsec a natural integration point for QKD in enterprise and telecom networks.
ProtocolOrToolImpact: MACsec; Layer 2 encryption; QKD key injection
